title: E-Commerce Inventory
description: Design an inventory management system for an e-commerce platform that handles concurrent stock reservations, prevents overselling, and syncs warehouse state.
requirements: Reserve stock atomically when order is placed (prevent overselling)
Release reserved stock if order payment fails or expires (15 min timeout)
Near-real-time inventory count visible to customers (eventual consistency ok)
Sync inventory updates from multiple warehouses
Support flash sales with high concurrency (10K concurrent add-to-cart)
nonFunctionalReqs: Zero overselling — strong consistency for reservations
< 100ms P99 for stock check and reservation
Handle 10K concurrent reservations per SKU during flash sales
Warehouse sync within 30 seconds of warehouse update

components.label: API Gateway
components.javaNote: Routes /inventory GET requests to Inventory Service and /orders POST to Order Service. Applies per-user rate limiting during flash sales to prevent single user from hogging reservations.

components.label: Inventory Service
components.javaNote: Core service. Two operations: checkStock (reads Redis) and reserveStock (optimistic lock on Postgres). Publishes StockUpdated events to Kafka for downstream consumers (search index, warehouse sync).

components.label: DB (Postgres)
components.javaNote: Table: inventory(sku_id PK, total_qty, reserved_qty, version). Optimistic locking: UPDATE inventory SET reserved_qty=reserved_qty+1, version=version+1 WHERE sku_id=? AND version=? AND total_qty-reserved_qty >= 1. Returns 0 rows on conflict → retry or reject.

components.label: Redis Cache
components.javaNote: Stores available_qty per SKU with 10s TTL. Read path hits Redis first. On cache miss: read from DB. On reservation: DECRBY in Redis + update DB. For flash sales: use Redis DECR as pre-check to reject early before DB contention.

components.label: Order Service
components.javaNote: Creates order, calls Inventory Service to reserve stock. Publishes OrderCreated to Kafka. Reservation expires after 15 min via Kafka Streams timer or scheduled job — releases stock back to Inventory Service.

components.label: Event Bus (Kafka)
components.javaNote: Topics: stock-updates (inventory changes), order-events (created/paid/expired/cancelled). Warehouse Worker consumes stock-updates to sync physical inventory. Order Service consumes order-events to auto-release expired reservations.

components.label: Warehouse Worker
components.javaNote: Kafka consumer that processes warehouse inventory updates (shipments received, returns processed). Uses UPSERT with version check to apply updates idempotently. Publishes reconciliation events on discrepancies.

componentBreakdown.heading: Optimistic Locking for Oversell Prevention
componentBreakdown.detail: Optimistic locking avoids pessimistic row-level locks (SELECT FOR UPDATE) which serialize all reservations. Instead, version column detects conflicts. On 0 rows updated: retry up to 3× or return 'out of stock'.

componentBreakdown.heading: Flash Sale: Redis Pre-Check
componentBreakdown.detail: During flash sales, DB becomes a bottleneck with thousands of concurrent optimistic lock retries. Use Redis DECR as a pre-check gate: only requests that successfully DECR (> 0) proceed to DB reservation. Reduces DB contention by 90%+.

componentBreakdown.heading: Reservation Expiry & Release
componentBreakdown.detail: Orders reserved but not paid within 15 minutes must release stock. Options: (1) Kafka Streams windowed aggregation, (2) scheduled job polling for expired reservations, (3) Redis TTL trigger via keyspace notifications.
